---
title: 'Dear Her 6/14/2020'
date: '2020-08-15T21:40:00.0000000+00:00'
draft: true
blogger_blog: '(My Journal) Fed By Faith'
blogger_status: 'LIVE'
blogger_post_id: '8988826524306557439'
blogger_filename: '/2020/08/dear-her-6142020.html'
blogger_source: 'takeout-20260223T235336Z-3-001.zip:Takeout/Blogger/Blogs/(My Journal) Fed By Faith/feed.atom'
categories:
  - 'Dear Her'
---

Dear Her,<div><br></div><div>I'm now nearly certain I've met you. Nearly. So many signals, so many confirmations. You didn't come in a package I expected. Some of the logistics still don't make sense. There's enough disconnect that I'm still watching, waiting, listening... But I'm pretty sure she's you.</div><div><br></div><div>On April 21, I uninstalled all the dating apps. I decided I was done with that project. Still ready to meet someone organically.&nbsp;</div><div><br></div><div>On April 22, I had a potent PTSD reaction to a Shelley. It confirmed, somehow, in my heart, that I was done with the apps but simultaneously reaffirmed for me my desire to meet someone who loves me well. I even pointed out that I'd like to meet someone who was like Theresa in many ways, but also equipped to be my partner which Theresa never will be.&nbsp;</div><div><br></div><div>Later that night, I met Jennifer Valentine Bradford Nelson.</div><div><br></div><div>The main thing she does, is the one thing nobody else has. She challenges me. Iron Sharpens Iron. And she sharpens me. Yes, other's show me ways I can grow. Even Theresa challenged me in a way, but, it was in a very "other" sort of way. She didn't speak my soul language.</div><div><br></div><div>Jen, she speaks my Daddy's heart aback to me, and sharpens my deepest soul places while simultaneously helping me feel safe to be me.&nbsp;</div><div><br></div><div>To top it off, the one thing that bothered me, "vegetarian", is now my doctor's orders to me. So it seems he's aligning us.</div><div><br></div><div>So we shall see, but, I believe this is the one I will build my life with.&nbsp;</div><div><br></div><div>My Life Partner.</div><div><br></div><div>It really comes down to, we are better together.&nbsp;</div><div><br></div><div>I refuse to rush this. I'm excited. I want her, in all ways, more now than ever. But. I want her in the right way. I will leave room for God to knitt our hearts and lives together, and set the timing of each phase.&nbsp;</div><div><br></div><div>I will follow him, and let him lead us, our kids, our families and friends, to see the same thing. I want our communities to agree too.&nbsp;</div><div><br></div><div><br></div><div><br></div><div><br></div><div><br></div><div><br></div><div><br></div><div><br></div><div><br></div><div><br></div><div><br></div><div><br></div><div><br></div><div><br></div><div><br></div><div><div class="separator" style="clear: both; text-align: center;">
  <a href="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/1592159365110651-0-9ef07e0d2b7e.png" imageanchor="1" style="margin-left: 1em; margin-right: 1em;">
    <img border="0"   src="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/1592159365110651-0-9ef07e0d2b7e.png" width="400">
  </a>
</div><br></div>
